A WEAK EF0 TORNADO, WHICH BEGAN IN SANDISFIELD, MOVED NORTHEAST INTO NORTHWEST TOLLAND AT 700 PM EDT AND LIFTED 20 MINUTES LATER IN SOUTHERN BLANDFORD AT 720 PM. IT WAS MAINLY CONTINUOUS FOR 7.8 MILES. MAX WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH. DOZENS OF TREES WERE DOWNED BUT NO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE OR INJURIES OCCURRED.

*** 5 INJ *** DEVENS FIRE CHIEF REPORTED 5 INJURIES FROM FT. DEVENS WHEN A MILITARY-GRADE TENT WAS BLOWN OVER BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS. THE 5 INJURED WERE TAKEN TO THE HOSPITAL AND TREATED FOR MINOR INJURIES. THEY HAVE SINCE BEEN RELEASED.

NWS SURVEY FOUND EF-0 TORNADO IN MILLIS, MA ON GROUND FOR LESS THAN 1 MINUTE, WITH PATH LENGTH OF 1/2 MILE, WIDTH 50 YARDS, AND MAXIMUM SPEED OF 75-80 MPH. BEGAN ON ORCHARD ST. WEST OF RIDGE ST. AND MOVED ESE TO OAK GROVE PLAYGROUND ON EXCHANGE ST. TOPS OF LARGE TREES SHEARED OFF AND EVIDENCE OF ROTATION.
